signals.reasoning: Existing ETH long has improved back into profit, is retaining 100% of current peak unrealized PnL, and the board remains CLEAR, so this is the cleanest thing to keep managing rather than exiting or rotating away from. The position has finally started working instead of remaining a cleanup problem.
BTC is broadly supportive but still not clearly stronger than simply managing the existing ETH long. With the board clear it stays constructive, just not enough to justify a switch.
SOL is moving with the same broad major-crypto tape and is not offering a better management profile than ETH. No rotation needed.
STBL may still be mechanically interesting, but it remains unstable enough that it does not outrank the current in-profit ETH exposure. Watch-only.
TST remains a noisy momentum name after its squeeze-style move, which keeps it below the bar for a management rotation away from ETH. Hold only.
AZTEC is still too fresh and execution-sensitive to justify switching away from a live ETH long that is finally working. Better watched than traded here.
VVV remains too unstable and reactive to trust as a cleaner alternative. The existing ETH position is simpler and stronger right now.
MAVIA can move sharply, but that volatility still makes it a lower-quality management switch than the current profitable ETH hold. Keep it on the watchlist.
CAKE may have constructive structure, but it does not clearly outrank the open ETH exposure that is now back in profit. No need to rotate.
LDO still has some constructive rebound structure, but not enough separation to justify leaving the existing ETH hold. Stay with ETH management.
MERL remains in rebound mode without becoming a clearly superior opportunity. It does not justify rotating away from the current ETH position.
